topic.name: Energy Policy and Incentives
topic.description: Legislation related to energy production, efficiency, and renewable resources, including tax credits and investment in clean technologies.

Document 1: tax credit for producing electricity from an Indian coal production facility
eliminate the requirement that such a facility be placed in service before January 1, 2009
Document 2: tax credit for research expenditures by 20% for qualified energy research expenses
research expenses related to fuel cell and battery technology, renewable energy
Document 3: authorize the Secretary of Energy to barter, transfer, or sell uranium
maintain a sufficient inventory of uranium to meet current and foreseeable needs
Document 4: replace at least 10% of the gasoline-powered motor vehicles in the Postal fleet with electric motor vehicles
ensure that at least 75% of the Postal fleet is comprised of such electric motor vehicles
Document 5: allow a tax credit for the purchase of a new qualified flexible fuel motor vehicle
flexible fuel vehicle certified by the Environmental Protection Agency
Document 6: establish a loan and grant program for photovoltaic solar installations
provide solar energy to low-income households
Document 7: authorize qualified industry organizations to conduct a referendum for the creation of a Carbon Storage Research Corporation
support large-scale demonstrations of carbon capture and storage technologies

topic.name: Invasive Species Management
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at preventing and managing the impact of invasive species on ecosystems and water resources.

Document 1: Invasive Species Emergency Response Fund
prevent and remediate the impacts of invasive species
Document 2: Stop Invasive Species Act
prevent the spread of aquatic nuisance species
Document 3: Invasive Species Emergency Response Fund
provide loans to qualified organizations to prevent and remediate the impacts of invasive species
Document 4: U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service
prevent, minimize, or abate such destruction, loss, or injury
Document 5: Clean Water Act State Revolving Fund
assist states in establishing simplified procedures for small systems
Document 6: fishery management plan for the commercial and recreational management
based on the best scientific information available
Document 7: tonnage requirement for consideration of dredging of small ports
minimum of 500,000 tons

topic.name: Medicare and Health Care Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative amendments and acts related to Medicare and health care policies in the United States.

Document 1: This bill amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act
study the treatment needs of individuals entitled to benefits under Medicare part A
Document 2: Family Health Care Flexibility Act - Repeals provisions of the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act
restrict payments from health savings accounts
Document 3: Strengthening Medicare Anti-Fraud Measures Act of 2011
authorize the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to exclude from participation in any federal health care program
Document 4: amends title I (Quality, Affordable Health Care for All Americans) of the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act
Individuals included in the above definition are eligible for special monthly enrollment periods on health insurance exchanges
Document 5: Amends part A (General Provisions) of title XI of the Social Security Act
make Guam and the Virgin Islands eligible for the Supplemental Security Income program
Document 6: Social Security COLA Protection Act of 2007 - Amends title XVIII (Medicare)
limit increases in the monthly Medicare premium
Document 7: Medicare Access to Community Health Centers (MATCH) Act of 2009
revises the definition of 'federally qualified health center services'

topic.name: Legislative Reforms and Rights
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative acts aimed at enhancing civil rights, workplace accommodations, and the integrity of democratic processes in the United States.

Document 1: Cedes the District of Columbia to Maryland after Maryland's acceptance of such retrocession.
Maintains the exclusive legislative authority and control of Congress over the Area.
Document 2: Amends provisions of the Civil Rights Act of 1964 related to equal employment opportunities.
Prohibits an accommodation from being a reasonable accommodation if it requires an employee to be segregated from customers or the general public.
Document 3: Prohibit the exclusion of citizens from service as a grand or petit juror on account of sexual orientation or gender identity.
Document 4: Amends the National Labor Relations Act to require the National Labor Relations Board to certify a bargaining representative without directing an election.
Adds to remedies for such violations: back pay plus liquidated damages.
Document 5: Amends the Help America Vote Act of 2002 to require each voting system to produce an individual permanent paper record for each ballot that is cast.
Document 6: Provides that federal departments, agencies, or courts must not prohibit, interfere with, regulate, or otherwise restrict the interstate traffic of unpasteurized milk.
Document 7: Authorizes the U.S. Tax Court to appoint a clerk of court and other necessary employees without regard to civil service provisions.

topic.name: Social Welfare and Support Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on various legislative acts aimed at improving social welfare, child protection, and community support services.

Document 1: amends the National Child Protection Act of 1993
establish policies and procedures to streamline the process of obtaining state and national criminal history background checks
Document 2: require a state or state-designated eligible entity to consult with each federally recognized Indian tribe
development or implementation of a statewide youth suicide early intervention and prevention strategy
Document 3: appropriates funds for assistance to eligible entities
redevelopment of abandoned and foreclosed-upon properties
Document 4: amends the Departments of Veterans Affairs and Housing and Urban Development
limits the payment standard used in connection with a voucher for tenant-based rental assistance
Document 5: reauthorize appropriations for a grant program to ensure the survival and continuing vitality of Native American languages
Document 6: make grants to state and local governments and Indian tribes to carry out employment training programs
assist long-term unemployed persons obtain the skills and training to re-enter the workforce
Document 7: directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services to award a grant to each state
carry out a program for making available throughout that state the 2-1-1 telephone service for information and referral on human services

topic.name: Environmental Regulation and Infrastructure
topic.description: The documents discuss legislative measures related to environmental regulations, infrastructure projects, and their implications on public safety and compliance.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Power Act to require the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission (FERC) to ensure that any emergency order issued... shall require generation, delivery, interchange, or transmission of electric energy only during hours necessary to meet the emergency and serve the public interest.
to the maximum extent practical, be consistent with any other applicable environmental law and minimize any adverse environmental impacts.
Document 4: Requires the Secretary of the Army to approve or disapprove a covered permit application for a public safety project within two years after the application is submitted.
weigh the public safety aspects of the project as greater than the environmental costs.
Document 6: Prohibits the Secretary of the Army and the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) from finalizing the proposed guidance regarding Identification of Waters Protected by the Clean Water Act.

topic.name: Regulatory Compliance and Oversight
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative measures aimed at enhancing regulatory compliance, oversight, and accountability in financial, electoral, and public safety sectors.

Document 1: Amends the Fair Credit Reporting Act with respect to the duties of users of consumer reports
Excludes any health care practice, accounting practice, or legal practice with 20 or fewer employees from the meaning of creditor
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of the Treasury to require reports concerning means of access to funds
Subjects violations of this Act to criminal and civil forfeiture
Document 3: Prohibits a national security letter from being issued unless the issuing official certifies specific facts
Provides a civil cause of action for the misuse of letters
Document 4: Establishes the Public Safety Interoperable Communications Working Group
Requires the FCC to complete a specified Notice of Proposed Rulemaking regarding the 700MHz Band
Document 5: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to require any person conducting a federal election phone bank to report to the Federal Election Commission
Limits such requirement to projects contacting at least 1,500 households during the 25 days before a federal election
Document 6: Requires the Internal Revenue Service to provide a substantive written response to any written correspondence from a taxpayer
to conclude any audit of an individual taxpayer not later than 1 year after the audit is initiated
Document 7: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to authorize the Secretary of the Treasury to disclose to any person who provides information indicating a violation of internal revenue laws
whether any action has been taken against a violator, including a referral for prosecution

topic.name: Financial Regulation and Consumer Protection
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the regulation of financial institutions and consumer protection in financial transactions.

Document 1: Amends the Bank Holding Company Act of 1956
prohibits financial holding companies and national banks from engaging in real estate brokerage or real estate management activities.
Document 2: Amends the Commodity Exchange Act to extend its coverage to specified energy commodities
establish position limits for energy and agricultural commodities.
Document 3: Amends the Electronic Fund Transfer Act with respect to mandatory fee disclosures
limits such requirement to appearance of a fee disclosure on the ATM screen.
Document 4: Amends the Consumer Credit Protection Act to prescribe consumer protection guidelines for rental-purchase transactions
grants enforcement powers to the Federal Trade Commission and to state attorneys general.
Document 5: Directs the federal banking agencies to study and report to Congress on the impact of the NPRs
evaluate their impact as well as the cumulative impact of the NPRs and the identified provisions on required regulatory capital levels.
Document 6: Amends the Truth in Lending Act to require a creditor to disclose to a consumer
increases the actual damages for which a creditor is liable for noncompliance.
Document 7: Amends the Federal Credit Union Act to permit an examination to be carried out only once every 18 months
the NCUA shall issue a report on how this bill affects its budget.

topic.name: U.S. Legislative Acts for Development and Safety
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various U.S. legislative acts aimed at enhancing energy efficiency, foreign assistance, disaster preparedness, and counterterrorism efforts.

Document 1: Offshore Wind Energy Research, Development, Demonstration, and Commercial Application Act of 2009
requires the Secretary of Energy to implement a program to improve energy efficiency, reliability, and capacity of offshore wind turbines.
Document 2: Countering Terrorist Radicalization Act
authorizes the Department of Homeland Security to provide training for administering community awareness briefings.
Document 3: Foreign Assistance Revitalization and Accountability Act of 2009
strengthen the capacity of the United States Agency for International Development (USAID) to establish development policies.
Document 4: Tsunami Forecasting and Warning Improvement Act of 2011
directs the Administrator of NOAA to establish or maintain a Caribbean Tsunami Warning Center.
Document 5: Vietnam Education Foundation Amendments Act of 2008
establishes the Vietnam Education Foundation within the Bureau of Educational and Cultural Affairs.
Document 6: Global Development Lab Act of 2014
establishes in USAID the United States Global Development Lab whose responsibilities should include increasing the application of science, technology, innovation.

topic.name: U.S. Postal Service Designations and Agricultural Policies
topic.description: This cluster discusses the designation of U.S. Postal Service facilities and legislative measures related to agricultural policies and food aid programs.

Document 1: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 1449 West Avenue in Bronx, New York, as the 'Private Isaac T. Cortes Post Office.'
Document 2: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 47 East Fayette Street in Uniontown, Pennsylvania, as the 'George C. Marshall Post Office.'
Document 4: Establishes the Food for Peace program in the U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID) under the Foreign Assistance Act of 1961.

topic.name: Labor and Benefits Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to labor rights, benefits for workers, and support for specific groups such as veterans and older workers.

Document 1: This bill amends the Black Lung Benefits Act to revise requirements for miners' claims for pneumoconiosis (black lung) benefits.
Labor must report to Congress a strategy to reduce the backlog of black lung cases pending before the Office of Administrative Law.
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow employers... a business-related tax credit for 25% of the first $6,000 of the wages of employees who have attained the age of 62.
Defines 'formal flexible work program' as a work program... that does not permit a change or reduction in the health care or pension benefits of the participating employee.
Document 3: Authorizes the Secretary of Veterans Affairs... to increase by up to $1,000 a month the rate of the Medal of Honor special pension.
Document 4: Directs the Secretary of Defense to issue guidance to ensure access to, and affordability of, respite care... for spouses of members of the Armed Forces deployed to a combat zone.
Document 5: Increases the annual rate of pension payable through the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) for disabled veterans who are married to one another and who both require regular aid and attendance.
Document 6: Directs the Secretary of Defense (DOD) to ensure that each of the following individuals receive... a telephone call from properly-trained DOD personnel to determine their emotional, psychological, medical, and career needs and concerns.
Document 7: Amends the Supplemental Appropriations Act, 2008 to revise the formula for Tier-1 amounts a state credits to an applicant's emergency unemployment compensation account (EUCA) for a benefit year.
Extends the period of emergency unemployment compensation through August 27, 2009.

topic.name: Health Care Legislation
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts aimed at improving health care services, education, and awareness for specific populations including veterans, individuals with disabilities, and those at risk for mental health issues.

Document 1: reauthorize through FY2019 the Young Women's Breast Health Education and Awareness Requires Learning Young Act
campaigns to educate the public and health care professionals about young women's breast health
Document 2: ensure the provision of services and treatment to each veteran enrolled in the VA health care system
screening for substance use disorder in all settings, including primary care
Document 3: require the Architectural and Transportation Barriers Compliance Board to issue and periodically review standards
promote good health, disease prevention, and wellness for individuals with disabilities
Document 4: develop and implement a plan for the prevention, control, and management of hepatitis C virus
support activities to promote the early detection of HCV infection
Document 5: reauthorize and revise a research, training, and technical assistance resource center to prevent suicides
enhance services for students with mental health or substance use disorders at institutions of higher education
Document 6: require the Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) to award matching grants
promote priority competencies that are selected annually by the Advisory Committee on Training in Primary Care Medicine and Dentistry
Document 7: include physical therapy within the definition of 'primary health services'
make physical therapists eligible for repayment of their educational loans

topic.name: Education Policy and Reform
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at improving educational access, quality, and outcomes across various levels of education.

Document 1: amends the Higher Education Act of 1965
requires job placement information for schools
Document 2: reauthorizes appropriations for adult education
awards grants for integrated English literacy programs
Document 3: directs the Secretary of Education to make grants to states
implements state middle grades needs analyses
Document 4: includes training of school personnel to recognize child sexual abuse
uses grants to states and local educational agencies
Document 5: provides additional school counselors for at-risk students
grantees must improve graduation rates
Document 6: requires information on transfer credits for college completion
mandates articulation agreements between public IHEs
Document 7: provides teacher certification stipends for armed forces veterans
allows teaching in high-need local educational agencies

topic.name: Land Management and Conveyance
topic.description: Legislation related to the management, designation, and conveyance of federal and state lands for various purposes.

Document 1: Amends the Cache La Poudre River Corridor Act to redesignate the Cache La Poudre Corridor as the 'Cache la Poudre River National Heritage Area.'
Requires the Alliance to prepare and submit a management plan for the Area.
Document 2: Directs the Administrator of General Services to ensure that the federal building located at McKinley Avenue and Third Street, S.W., Canton, Ohio, is designated as the 'Ralph Regula Federal Building.'
Document 3: Directs the Attorney General to convey to Miami Dade College in Miami Dade County, Florida, a parcel of land held by the Bureau of Prisons for a new educational building.
Document 4: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to transfer to the Secretary of the Navy, without consideration, approximately 400 acres of federal land.
Document 5: Amends the National Children's Island Act of 1995 to allow the District of Columbia to use the lands conveyed for recreational, environmental, or educational purposes.
States that title in the Islands and the related easements shall revert back to the United States 60 days after the Secretary of Interior notifies the District.
Document 6: Directs the Secretary of Agriculture (USDA) to convey and quitclaim all interest of the United States in and to a parcel of real property.
Document 7: Replaces the map subtitled 'Sachuest Point Unit RI-04P' included in the set of maps entitled 'John H. Chafee Coastal Barrier Resources System.'

topic.name: Veteran and Rural Small Business Support
topic.description: Legislative measures aimed at enhancing financial assistance and opportunities for veteran-owned and rural small businesses.

Document 1: Establish an application process for rural area designation under federal consumer financial law.
Criteria for evaluating rural area applications.
Document 2: Reinstates the veteran-owned small business loan program.
Increases maximum loan guaranty amount from $200,000 to $500,000.
Document 3: Makes small businesses owned by service-disabled veterans eligible for small business development grants.
Aligns eligibility with socially and economically disadvantaged individuals.
Document 4: Includes Campbell County, Virginia, as a qualified nonmetropolitan county for HUBZone programs.
Document 5: Permits federal agencies to count contracts with small business teaming arrangements toward procurement goals.
Includes HUBZone small businesses and those owned by veterans and disadvantaged individuals.
Document 6: Reinstates the VA small business loan program for veterans.
Increases loan guaranty amount and provides preferences for National Guard and reserve members.
Document 7: Increases the maximum amount of a loan under the microloan program from $50,000 to $100,000.
Authorizes the SBA to educate about the microloan program.

topic.name: U.S. Immigration Reform
topic.description: Legislative efforts aimed at reforming various aspects of U.S. immigration policy, including temporary protected status, asylum procedures, and visa allocations.

Document 1: amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to revise the criteria for designation of a foreign state as eligible to have its citizens be given temporary protected status
an alien lacking lawful immigration status shall be ineligible for TPS
Document 2: amends the Immigration and Naturalization Act to eliminate the one-year time limit for filing an asylum claim
sets forth protections for minors, refugees, aliens interdicted at sea, and stateless persons
Document 3: adjust the status of an eligible alien entrepreneur, alien spouse, or alien child to that of an alien lawfully admitted for permanent residency
requires that an eligible alien: became a conditional resident as an alien entrepreneur
Document 4: initial 36-month admission period for a nonimmigrant alien employed as a dairy worker under an H-2A visa
Document 5: amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to include business facilitation visitors from a qualifying country
defines 'business facilitation visitor' as an alien visiting the United States temporarily for business
Document 6: amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to make up to 55,000 visas available to qualified immigrants
requires the Department of Homeland Security
Document 7: expresses the sense of Congress that the United States must address the violence and humanitarian crisis
increased resources for, and expedited processing under, the Central American Minors (CAM) program

topic.name: Criminal Justice Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on recent U.S. legislative measures aimed at enhancing criminal penalties, judicial procedures, and protections for victims, particularly concerning violent crimes and offenses against children.

Document 1: Amends the federal criminal code to increase or impose new criminal penalties for the use of firearms to commit a crime of violence
Imposes a minimum mandatory prison term for possession of child pornography.
Document 2: Amends the Uniform Code of Military Justice (UCMJ) to repeal the authority of a convening authority to use discretion to either set aside a finding of guilty
Prohibits a convening authority from reducing a sentence to less than the mandatory minimum sentence.
Document 4: Amends the federal judicial code to require the Director of the U.S. Marshals Service to consult with the Judicial Conference of the United States regarding the security requirements of the judicial branch
Authorizes an additional appropriation for the U.S. Marshals Service for protection of the judiciary.
Document 5: Directs the Attorney General to establish and maintain a database solely for collecting DNA information with respect to predators against children
Requires the maximum sentence to be imposed for a crime against an individual under age 18 that would establish the offender as a predator.
Document 6: Amends the National Child Protection Act of 1993 to direct the Attorney General to establish a program for national criminal history background checks for child-serving organizations
Requires the applicant processing center to complete criminal background checks within 10 business days after receiving a request.

topic.name: Federal Funding and Assistance Programs
topic.description: This cluster discusses various federal acts and programs aimed at providing financial assistance and support across different sectors, including unemployment, energy, transportation, and health.

Document 1: Unemployment Supplemental Assistance Act - Provides for federal-state agreements for increased regular unemployment compensation payments to individuals.
Requires federal payments to states to cover 100% of such additional payments.
Document 2: Establishes the Energy Security Fund, to be funded by proceeds deposited in the general fund of the Treasury from fines, penalties, and other funds obtained through enforcement actions for violations of automobile fuel economy standards.
Directs the Secretary of Energy, acting through the Clean Cities Program of the Department of Energy, to establish a program of grants to expand the availability of alternative fuels to consumers.
Document 3: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to extend through September 16, 2011, increased excise taxes on aviation fuels, the excise tax on air transportation of persons and property, and the expenditure authority for the Airport and Airway Trust Fund.

Increases the authorization of appropriations for the period beginning on October 1, 2010, and ending on September 16, 2011, for airport planning and development and noise compatibility planning projects.
Document 4: Extends the authorization of appropriations for the port security grant program through FY2017.
Document 5: Requires specified funds to be transferred to the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) from discretionary amounts appropriated under the American Recovery and Reinvestment Act that remain unobligated, to be obligated during the FY2010 grant period under the AIDS Drug Assistance Program.
Requires amounts transferred under this Act to be used by states in a manner consistent with their state programs.
Document 6: This bill prohibits the availability of federal funds to the Metropolitan Washington Airports Authority unless the Department of Transportation certifies that it is complying with appropriate auditing standards.
Document 7: Appropriates, out of any moneys of the District of Columbia government not otherwise appropriated, and out of applicable corporate or other revenues, receipts, and funds, the amount provided for any project or activity for which funds are provided in the local budget act for such fiscal year.

topic.name: Government Accountability and Oversight
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at enhancing government accountability, efficiency, and safety through oversight and regulatory measures.

Document 1: Protecting American Taxpayers from Fraud Act of 2015
ensure that there is coordination among the following Internal Revenue Service (IRS) entities
Document 2: establishes the Government Transformation Board

review programs and conduct research to determine whether the organizational practices, operations, and reform efforts of federal agencies are useful, duplicative, efficient, effective, and economical
Document 3: directs the Secretary of Homeland Security to conduct a survey
develop a plan to enhance firefighter safety
Document 4: establish and administer a system to identify members of the Armed Forces who were potentially exposed to a hazardous disposal site
determine, and report to Congress on, whether existing surveillance systems are sufficient
Document 5: establishes the Office of Regulatory Analysis
submit by January 30 of each year to the House Committees on Oversight and Government Reform
Document 6: require the Secretary of the Interior to make available online a searchable database detailing federal expenditures
submit an annual report to Congress including this information
Document 7: prohibits funds appropriated or otherwise made available to the Secretary of the Treasury
establishes a Social Security Benefits Protection from Attachment or Garnishment Advisory Committee

topic.name: U.S. Military Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to the policies, funding, and operational aspects of the U.S. Armed Forces.

Document 1: Repeal of the current Department of Defense (DOD) policy concerning homosexuality in the Armed Forces
implementation of such policies and regulations is consistent with military readiness
Document 2: prohibits the use of a military installation to house any alien unlawfully present in the United States
Document 3: Prohibits Department of Defense (DOD) funds for FY2012-FY2013 from being used to provide the Russian Federation with access to U.S. missile defense technology
directs the President to provide annual notification to Congress regarding missile defense capabilities
Document 4: Directs the President to close the Department of Defense (DOD) detention facility at Guantanamo Bay, Cuba
Prohibits use of funds appropriated for FY2007 or FY2008 for such facility
Document 5: Reauthorizes and amends authorities relating to the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration's Commissioned Officer Corps
Maintains the existing distribution percentages of officers on the lineal list in grades of captain, commander, and lieutenant commander
Document 6: Makes enlisted members of the Army, Navy, Marine Corps, and Coast Guard eligible to participate in higher education programs
Currently, only enlisted members of the Air Force are eligible
Document 7: Amends the Military Construction Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2007
includes authorized Army military construction projects at Fort Lee, Virginia, and Fort Lewis, Washington

topic.name: U.S. Tariff Amendments
topic.description: Legislative amendments related to the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States focusing on temporary duty suspensions and reductions.

Document 1: reducing temporarily the duty on arrangements of artificial flowers of man-made fibers.
Document 3: extend the temporary suspension of duty on mixtures of insecticide.
Document 4: suspend temporarily the duty on knit tank tops of stretch fabric with textile or polymer-based electrodes.
Document 5: extend the temporary suspension of duty on methyl salicylate.
Document 6: extend temporarily the suspension of duty on Reactive Blue 224.
Document 7: provide duty-free treatment for certain federal property returned to the United States.

topic.name: Aviation and Drug Regulation
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses legislation related to aviation safety and drug regulation in the United States.

Document 1: prohibit the use of leaded fuel by aircraft
FAA regulations
Document 2: apportion amounts for airport planning and development
noise compatibility planning
Document 4: installation of secondary barriers to prevent access to the flight deck
locked cockpit doors during flight
Document 5: amendment or repeal of an over-the-counter drug monograph
FDA to regulate over-the-counter drug advertisements
Document 6: abbreviated application for a new drug
market exclusivity for pediatric studies
Document 7: security measures at Cuba's international airports
Federal Air Marshal Service missions on flights

topic.name: Hydraulic Fracturing and Environmental Regulation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation and regulatory measures related to hydraulic fracturing, oil and gas extraction, and environmental protection.

Document 1: This bill repeals the exemption for hydraulic fracturing operations relating to oil and natural gas production activities under the Safe Drinking Water Act.
The chemicals intended for use in underground injections must be disclosed before the hydraulic fracturing operations commence.
Document 2: Requires the Administrator of the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) to conduct and report to Congress on... the efficient and environmentally sound recovery of minerals, other than oil and natural gas, from the shallow and deep seabed of the United States.
Document 3: Amends the Mineral Leasing Act to prohibit a lessee from conducting any activity under the lease for the purpose of hydraulic fracturing.
Document 5: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to eliminate... the $1 billion per incident limitation on expenditures from the Oil Spill Liability Trust Fund for cleanup of oil spills.
Document 6: Amends the Oil Pollution Act of 1990 to require the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to enter into an arrangement with the National Academies to evaluate and report on the natural resource damages from the blowout and explosion of the mobile offshore drilling unit Deepwater Horizon.

topic.name: Student Loan Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative acts aimed at regulating and reforming student loans and financing in higher education.

Document 1: Amends the Truth in Lending Act to require a lender, before issuing a private education loan for a student attending an institution of higher education, to obtain the IHE's certification.
Requires private educational lenders to send loan statements to borrowers at least once every three months.
Document 5: Amends title IV of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to direct the Secretary of Education to establish a program to refinance the unpaid principal, accrued unpaid interest, and late charges on Federal Direct Loans.
Sets the interest rate on the refinanced loans based on the DL's categorization.

topic.name: Tax Code Amendments for Businesses
topic.description: Legislative changes to the Internal Revenue Code aimed at providing tax benefits and credits to various types of businesses and economic sectors.

Document 1: denies a tax deduction for excess reinsurance premiums
paid to affiliated insurance companies
Document 2: allows a business-related tax credit for 35% of new investment
defines 'rural microbusiness' as a trade or business that employs not more than 5 full-time employees
Document 3: allows a five-year carryback of net operating losses
including the operating losses of life insurance companies
Document 4: extends through 2018 the tax credit for differential wage payments
made by a small business employer
Document 5: increases the estate tax exclusion to $5 million
imposes a maximum estate tax rate of 45%
Document 6: excludes from gross income payments received by a student
under a comprehensive student work-learning-service program
Document 7: allows small business employers with an average of 500 or fewer employees
a partially refundable tax credit for 20% of their research expenditures
